description: Schedule parameters and calendar rules. Load on-demand during planning.

timezone.dst_rule: Last Sunday of March → CEST; Last Sunday of October → CET
timezone.conversion_reminder: ALWAYS convert Outlook UTC → Berlin. Double-check each event lands on the correct day and hour.

calendar_rules.immovable: Accepted and organizer events. Never schedule over them.
calendar_rules.tentative: Show with (tentative) marker. Can schedule tasks in the slot if needed, flagging conflict.
calendar_rules.gtd_category: Calendar events categorized as 'GTD' are previous time-blocks. Treat as available time — do NOT display as meetings.
calendar_rules.buffer: 5-minute gaps between blocks for context switching.

capacity.rule: Fill ~80% of available time. Leave 20% buffer for overflow and context switching.

task_nature_categories.name: follow-ups
task_nature_categories.batching: Group all follow-ups into one block

task_nature_categories.name: compliance
task_nature_categories.batching: Batch all compliance into one slot

task_nature_categories.name: admin
task_nature_categories.batching: End of day or gaps between meetings

meeting_task_matching.rule: Tasks tagged with a person name should surface as 💬 bring-up items ONLY during 1-on-1 meetings with that person. Never during large group meetings.
meeting_task_matching.matching: Match task tags (lowercase) against meeting attendee email prefixes (e.g., tag 'malte' → malte.kalkoffen@endios.de). Also match task title keywords against meeting subject.
meeting_task_matching.skipped_1on1: If a 1-on-1 is skipped, use AskUserQuestion to ask about EACH tagged task individually — defer to next week, handle async, or bring up in a different meeting.
